Name: Martyrs' Memorial
Description: An iconic towering concrete monument celebrating Algeria's independence.

Name: Botanical Garden Hamma
Description: A lush and historic botanical garden established in 1832 featuring thousands of plant species.

Name: Casbah
Description: A historic UNESCO World Heritage citadel characterized by its narrow winding streets and rich history.

Name: Great Mosque
Description: A modern architectural marvel boasting the world's tallest minaret.

Name: Notre Dame d'Afrique
Description: A stunning Catholic basilica from the 19th century offering panoramic views of the Bay of Algiers.

Name: Bardo National Museum
Description: A fascinating museum of prehistory and ethnography housed in a beautifully restored Moorish villa.

Name: National Museum of Fine Arts
Description: One of the largest art museums in Africa featuring Algerian and European masterpieces.

Name: Palais des Raïs
Description: A beautifully preserved Ottoman-era palace complex sitting right on the Mediterranean coast.

Name: Ketchaoua Mosque
Description: A historic mosque blending Moorish and Byzantine architecture located at the foot of the Casbah.

Name: La Grande Poste
Description: A magnificent neo-Moorish post office that serves as a central landmark in downtown Algiers.

Name: Museum of Antiquities and Islamic Arts
Description: The oldest museum in Algeria showcasing ancient mosaics and beautiful Islamic artifacts.

Name: Martyrs' Square
Description: A bustling public square steeped in history and offering great views of the sea.

Name: National Museum of Moudjahid
Description: Located beneath the Martyrs' Memorial it details the Algerian struggle for independence.

Name: Dar Mustapha Pacha
Description: An exquisite 18th-century Ottoman palace renowned for its interior tile work.

Name: Museum of Modern Art
Description: A hub for contemporary Algerian art located in a beautifully restored neo-Moorish commercial building.

Name: Djamaa el Djedid
Description: A 17th-century Ottoman mosque with striking white domes prominently situated near the harbor.

Name: Ali La Pointe Museum
Description: A poignant memorial dedicated to the famed Algerian martyr located deep within the Casbah.

Name: Ben Aknoun Zoo
Description: A sprawling zoo and amusement park perfect for family outings.

Name: Liberty Park
Description: A quiet geometrical park offering scenic stairs greenery and Ottoman-inspired ceramics.

Name: Museum of Popular Arts and Traditions
Description: Housed in a 16th-century palace this museum displays traditional Algerian crafts and jewelry.

Name: Dar Aziza
Description: One of the oldest surviving palaces in the Casbah featuring classic courtyard architecture.

Name: Dar Hassan Pacha
Description: A majestic former winter palace known for its fusion of Moorish and European styles.

Name: Emir Abdelkader Square
Description: A vibrant central plaza featuring a prominent statue of the famous Algerian leader.

Name: Port of Algiers
Description: The historic and modern maritime heart of the city.

Name: Sablettes Promenade
Description: A highly popular waterfront park area with walking paths playgrounds and sea views.

Name: Sidi Fredj Marina
Description: A picturesque marina and historical peninsula perfect for coastal dining and boat watching.

Name: Musée Central de l'Armée
Description: A massive military museum featuring historical artifacts tanks and aircraft located near the Martyrs' Memorial.

Name: Zeralda Beach
Description: A spacious sandy beach offering numerous tourist complexes and relaxation spots.

Name: Ain Benian Coast
Description: A rocky and scenic coastal stretch known for excellent seafood restaurants.

Name: Palais des Expositions (SAFEX)
Description: The sprawling main venue for international trade fairs auto shows and major cultural events.

Name: Cathédrale du Sacré-Cœur
Description: A striking modern cathedral built in the 1950s designed to look like a cooling tower or a nomad's tent.

Name: Tifariti Park
Description: A lush urban green space offering a tranquil escape from the bustling city.

Name: Beirut Garden
Description: A well-shaded family-friendly park known for its old trees and peaceful atmosphere.

Name: Dounia Park
Description: A massive developing ecological park ideal for long walks and outdoor activities.

Name: Palais de la Culture Moufdi Zakaria
Description: A prominent cultural center hosting exhibitions and events with breathtaking views of the bay.

Name: Musée National de la Marine
Description: A fascinating museum located in the historic Amirauté building showcasing Algeria's rich naval history.

Name: National Theatre
Description: A stunning performing arts venue located in a historic building.

Name: Safir Mosque
Description: A historic mosque situated within the winding alleys of the Casbah.

Name: Sidi Ramadan Mosque
Description: Believed to be one of the oldest mosques in the Casbah area.

Name: Ali Bitchin Mosque
Description: A 17th-century mosque built by an Italian convert with a fascinating backstory.

Name: El Barani Mosque
Description: A beautiful historical mosque located near the entrance of the Casbah citadel.

Name: Ben Farès Mosque
Description: Originally the Great Synagogue of Algiers before being converted into a mosque.

Name: Ryadh El Feth
Description: A vibrant commercial and cultural complex surrounding the Martyrs' Memorial.

Name: Hydra Square
Description: The upscale center of the Hydra district known for chic cafes and restaurants.

Name: El Kettani Beach
Description: A popular urban beach located in the Bab El Oued neighborhood.

Name: Bouchaoui Forest
Description: A dense and highly popular forest area used by locals for jogging and picnics.

Name: Bainem Forest
Description: The largest forest in the Algiers province offering excellent hiking trails.

Name: Bab Azzoun Street
Description: A famous historic shopping street lined with French-colonial arcades.

Name: Didouche Mourad Street
Description: The vibrant commercial artery of Algiers featuring stunning Haussmannian architecture.

Name: Audin Square
Description: A central and busy intersection famous for its bookstores and student life.

Name: Algiers Opera House
Description: A modern state-of-the-art venue hosting symphonies and theatrical performances.

Name: National Library of Algeria
Description: A massive cultural institution with extensive archives overlooking the bay.

Name: Monument to the Dead
Description: A solemn monument paying tribute to those who lost their lives in World War I.

Name: City Center Mall
Description: A modern retail and lifestyle complex offering a variety of international and local stores, diverse dining options, and a large Carrefour hypermarket

Name: Cinéma Algeria
Description: A beautifully renovated historic cinema located right in the heart of the capital on the famous Rue Didouche Mourad, known for screening both recent Algerian and international films.

Name: Sidi Abdellah Water Park
Description: A family-friendly aquatic and amusement park located in the Mahelma (Sidi Abdellah) area, offering swimming pools and water slides to cool off during the hot summer months

Name: Garden City Mall
Description: A beautifully designed modern open-air shopping and lifestyle center located in Cheraga.

Name: Ardis Mall
Description: A massive commercial center featuring retail stores a hypermarket and a waterfront promenade.

Name: Bab Ezzouar Mall
Description: One of the largest and most popular enclosed shopping and entertainment centers in Algiers.

Name: Dar Abd-el-Tif
Description: A magnificent Moorish mansion historically known for hosting resident French artists.

Name: Djamaa El Kebir
Description: The oldest mosque in Algiers, an architectural marvel originally built in 1097.

Name: Place Port Said
Description: A picturesque square located directly in front of the National Theater.

Name: Reghaia Lake Nature Reserve
Description: A vital coastal wetland and lake providing a habitat for migratory bird species.

Name: Balcon Saint-Raphaël
Description: A spectacular cliffside viewing terrace in El Biar offering the best panoramas of Algiers.

Name: Kiffan Club
Description: A long-standing, popular amusement and water park located in Bordj El Kiffan.

Name: Club des Pins
Description: A highly secure, exclusive beach and residential estate hosting diplomats and officials.
